data.content:
### Generate Auth Confirmation Link with Supabase-JS Admin API Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/www/_blog/2021-07-28-supabase-auth-passwordless-sms-login.mdx This code snippet illustrates how to generate various authentication links (e.g., invite, confirmation, recovery, magic) using the `supabase-js` admin API's `generateLink` method. This functionality requires a `service_role` admin key and should only be invoked from a secure backend environment, not directly from a client application. ```javascript const { data, error } = await supabase.auth.api.generateLink( 'invite', 'user@email.com', { redirectTo: 'http://localhost:3000/welcome' } ) ``` -------------------------------- ### Add admin role claim to user token in SQL Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/auth/auth-hooks/custom-access-token-hook.mdx This SQL function implements a custom access token hook that grants an 'admin' claim to users marked as administrators in a profiles table. It checks the is_admin flag and conditionally adds the admin claim to app_metadata. Requires a profiles table with user_id and is_admin columns. ```sql create table profiles ( user_id uuid not null primary key references auth.users (id), is_admin boolean not null default false ); ``` ```sql create or replace function public.custom_access_token_hook(event jsonb) returns jsonb language plpgsql as $$ declare claims jsonb; is_admin boolean; begin -- Check if the user is marked as admin in the profiles table select is_admin into is_admin from profiles where user_id = (event->>'user_id')::uuid; -- Proceed only if the user is an admin if is_admin then claims := event->'claims'; -- Check if 'app_metadata' exists in claims if jsonb_typeof(claims->'app_metadata') is null then -- If 'app_metadata' does not exist, create an empty object claims := jsonb_set(claims, '{app_metadata}', '{}'); end if; -- Set a claim of 'admin' claims := jsonb_set(claims, '{app_metadata, admin}', 'true'); -- Update the 'claims' object in the original event event := jsonb_set(event, '{claims}', claims); end if; -- Return the modified or original event return event; end; $$; grant all on table public.profiles to supabase_auth_admin; revoke all on table public.profiles from authenticated, anon, public; ``` -------------------------------- ### Verify Organization Admin RLS Privileges (SQL) Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/local-development/testing/pgtap-extended.mdx This test case authenticates as an 'org_admin' and verifies their ability to view all organization members, ensuring RLS policies correctly grant read access for administrators. It uses `results_eq` to assert the count of members visible to the admin. ```sql -- Test admin privileges select tests.authenticate_as('org_admin'); select results_eq( $$select count(*) from org_members$$, ARRAY[6::bigint], 'Admin can view all members' ); ``` -------------------------------- ### Hide Navigation Links in Supabase Auth UI (React) Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/auth/auth-helpers/auth-ui.mdx This snippet demonstrates how to prevent the Supabase Auth UI component from displaying default navigation links (e.g., 'Sign up', 'Forgot password') by setting the `showLinks` prop to `false`. It requires initializing a Supabase client and rendering the `Auth` component within a React application. ```javascript import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js' import { Auth } from '@supabase/auth-ui-react' const supabase = createClient('', '') const App = () => ``` -------------------------------- ### Create Postgres Function for Custom JWT Claim in Supabase Auth Hook Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/www/_blog/2023-12-14-supabase-auth-identity-linking-hooks.mdx This PL/pgSQL function demonstrates how to create an Auth Hook to inject a custom 'level' claim into a user's JWT. It queries the 'profiles' table to retrieve the user's level and then modifies the `event.claims` JSON object to include this data before the access token is generated, enabling dynamic customization of JWT contents. ```sql create function custom_access_token_hook(event jsonb) returns jsonb language plpgsql as $$ declare user_level jsonb; begin -- fetch the current user's level select to_jsonb(level) into user_level from profiles where user_id = event->>'user_id'::uuid; -- change the event.claims.level return jsonb_set( event, '{claims,level}', user_level); end; $$ ``` -------------------------------- ### Configure PKCE authentication flow with email redirect in Supabase Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/auth/auth-helpers/sveltekit.mdx Configures Supabase authentication to use the PKCE (Proof Key for Code Exchange) flow by setting emailRedirectTo to the code exchange route handler. This is required for server-side auth flows in SvelteKit Auth Helpers version 0.10 and above, directing users to the callback endpoint after email verification. ```typescript await supabase.auth.signUp({ email: 'valid.email@supabase.io', password: 'sup3rs3cur3', options: { emailRedirectTo: 'http://localhost:3000/auth/callback', }, }) ``` -------------------------------- ### Link OAuth Identity with Supabase Auth (JavaScript) Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/www/_blog/2023-12-14-supabase-auth-identity-linking-hooks.mdx This snippet demonstrates how to link an OAuth identity, such as Google, to a currently signed-in user's account using the `linkIdentity()` method. It requires the user to be authenticated and manual linking to be enabled in the Supabase dashboard. ```jsx const { data, error } = await supabase.auth.linkIdentity({ provider: 'google', }) ``` -------------------------------- ### Generate Supabase Auth Confirmation URL in JavaScript Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/auth/auth-hooks/send-email-hook.mdx Creates verification URLs for Supabase authentication actions by constructing a URL with token hash, email action type, and redirect parameters. This function is used to generate clickable links in authentication emails that users receive for email confirmation, password reset, and other auth flows. ```javascript function generateConfirmationURL(email_data) { // TODO: replace the ref with your project ref return `https://.supabase.co/auth/v1/verify?token=${email_data.token_hash}&type=${email_data.email_action_type}&redirect_to=${email_data.redirect_to}` } ``` -------------------------------- ### List All Users via Auth Admin API (v2) Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/www/_blog/2022-08-16-supabase-js-v2.mdx This `supabase-js v2` snippet shows how to list all users using the new `supabase.auth.admin.listUsers()` method. This method is part of the server-side Auth Admin API and explicitly requires a `SERVICE_ROLE` key, making it clear that it should only be used in trusted server environments. ```ts // v2 const { data: user, error } = await supabase.auth.admin.listUsers() ``` -------------------------------- ### Create Supabase User with Admin API (TypeScript) Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/platform/migrating-to-supabase/auth0.mdx This snippet demonstrates how to migrate existing users to Supabase Auth using the `supabase.auth.admin.createUser` method. It provides examples for creating users with either a pre-hashed password (supporting bcrypt and Argon2) or a plaintext password, which Supabase will automatically hash. ```typescript import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js' const supabase = createClient('your_project_url', 'your_supabase_api_key') // ---cut--- const { data, error } = await supabase.auth.admin.createUser({ email: 'valid.email@supabase.io', password_hash: '$2y$10$a9pghn27d7m0ltXvlX8LiOowy7XfFw0hW0G80OjKYQ1jaoejaA7NC', email_confirm: true, }) ``` ```typescript import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js' const supabase = createClient('your_project_url', 'your_supabase_api_key') // ---cut--- const { data, error } = await supabase.auth.admin.createUser({ email: 'valid.email@supabase.io', password: 'supersecurepassword123!', }) ``` -------------------------------- ### Grant USAGE Permission on Schema for Supabase Auth Admin (SQL) Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/auth/auth-hooks.mdx This SQL command grants `USAGE` permission on the `public` schema to the `supabase_auth_admin` role. This allows the authentication system to access and interact with database objects, including custom hook functions, residing within that schema. ```sql grant usage on schema public to supabase_auth_admin; ``` -------------------------------- ### Generate Supabase email OTP link for direct login (HTML) Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/auth/auth-email-templates.mdx This HTML snippet creates a direct confirmation link for email OTP (One-Time Password) based authentication. Clicking it immediately consumes the token for login. It's suitable when email prefetching is not a concern or for custom login flows. ```html Confirm your signup ``` -------------------------------- ### Configure SvelteKit Hooks for Supabase Authentication Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/auth/auth-helpers/sveltekit.mdx This snippet illustrates how to set up server-side hooks for Supabase authentication in SvelteKit. In older versions, `handleAuth` from `@supabase/auth-helpers-sveltekit` was used with a separate `getSession` function. Version 0.7.0 simplifies this by using `auth()` from `@supabase/auth-helpers-sveltekit/server` directly within `hooks.server.ts`, with an optional `sequence` for additional handlers. ```ts import { handleAuth } from '@supabase/auth-helpers-sveltekit' import type { GetSession, Handle } from '@sveltejs/kit' import { sequence } from '@sveltejs/kit/hooks' export const handle: Handle = sequence(...handleAuth()) export const getSession: GetSession = async (event) => { const { user, accessToken, error } = event.locals return { user, accessToken, error, } } ``` ```ts // make sure the supabase instance is initialized on the server import '$lib/db' import { dev } from '$app/environment' import { auth } from '@supabase/auth-helpers-sveltekit/server' export const handle = auth() ``` ```ts // make sure the supabase instance is initialized on the server import '$lib/db' import { dev } from '$app/environment' import { auth } from '@supabase/auth-helpers-sveltekit/server' import { sequence } from '@sveltejs/kit/hooks' export const handle = sequence(auth(), yourHandler) ``` -------------------------------- ### Verify OTP Token Hash - JavaScript Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/auth/auth-email-passwordless.mdx Exchanges token hash received from Magic Link email for authenticated session. Used in PKCE flow after user clicks Magic Link. Requires token_hash and type parameters for verification. ```javascript import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js' const supabase = createClient('url', 'anonKey') const { error } = await supabase.auth.verifyOtp({ token_hash: 'hash', type: 'email', }) ``` -------------------------------- ### Create Supabase Auth User with Metadata (TypeScript) Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/platform/migrating-to-supabase/auth0.mdx This TypeScript example demonstrates how to use the `supabase.auth.admin.createUser` method to register a new user. It shows how to assign `user_metadata` for public, user-editable data like `full_name` and `app_metadata` for private, admin-controlled data such as `role`, which are accessible via admin methods. This method requires an initialized Supabase client. ```typescript import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js' const supabase = createClient('your_project_url', 'your_supabase_api_key') const { data, error } = await supabase.auth.admin.createUser({ email: 'valid.email@supabase.io', user_metadata: { full_name: 'Foo Bar', }, app_metadata: { role: 'admin', }, }) ``` -------------------------------- ### Sign in with phone using OTP in Supabase Auth Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/docs/ref/javascript/v1/upgrade-guide.mdx This section demonstrates the migration for signing in a user with a phone number using a One-Time Password (OTP) in Supabase. The 'Before' example shows the deprecated `sendMobileOTP` method, while the 'After' example introduces `signInWithOtp` for sending the OTP and `verifyOtp` for verifying it, aligning with the updated API. ```ts const { error } = await supabase .auth .api .sendMobileOTP(phone) ``` ```ts const { data, error } = await supabase .auth .signInWithOtp({ phone }) // After receiving a SMS with a OTP. const { data, error } = await supabase .auth .verifyOtp({ phone, token }) ``` -------------------------------- ### Generate HTML for Magic Link Login Email Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/auth/auth-hooks/send-email-hook.mdx This snippet provides localized HTML templates for magic link login emails, allowing users to log in without a password. It includes a direct login link and an alternative token. Placeholders `{{confirmation_url}}` and `{{token}}` are dynamically populated. ```html

Magic Link

Follow this link to login:

Log In

Alternatively, enter the code: {{token}}

``` ```html

Tu enlace mágico

Sigue este enlace para iniciar sesión:

Iniciar sesión

Alternativamente, ingresa el código: {{token}}

``` ```html

Votre Lien Magique

Suivez ce lien pour vous connecter :

Connectez-vous

Vous pouvez aussi saisir le code : {{token}}

``` -------------------------------- ### Verify phone number with an SMS OTP using Supabase Auth Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/auth/passwords.mdx If phone verification is enabled, users receive a 6-digit SMS pin after signing up. This code shows how to verify that pin by calling `verifyOtp()` (or its language-specific equivalent) with the user's phone number, the received token, and the type of verification ('sms'). The pin must be verified within 60 seconds. ```JavaScript import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js' const supabase = createClient('https://your-project.supabase.co', 'sb_publishable_... or anon key') // ---cut--- const { data: { session }, error, } = await supabase.auth.verifyOtp({ phone: '+13334445555', token: '123456', type: 'sms', }) ``` ```Swift try await supabase.auth.verifyOTP( phone: "+13334445555", token: "123456", type: .sms ) ``` ```Kotlin supabase.auth.verifyPhoneOtp( type = OtpType.Phone.SMS, phone = "+13334445555", token = "123456" ) ``` ```Python supabase.auth.verify_otp({ 'phone': "+13334445555", 'token': "123456", 'type': "sms" }) ``` ```Dart final AuthResponse res = await supabase.auth.verifyOTP( phone: '+13334445555', token: '123456', type: OtpType.sms, ); ``` ```HTTP curl -X POST 'https://.supabase.co/auth/v1/verify' \ -H "apikey: " \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{ "type": "sms", "phone": "+13334445555", "token": "123456" }' ``` -------------------------------- ### Apply predefined theme to Supabase Auth UI component in React Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/auth/auth-helpers/auth-ui.mdx To style the Auth component, import a predefined theme like `ThemeSupa` from `@supabase/auth-ui-shared`. This theme is then applied by passing it to the `appearance.theme` prop of the `Auth` component, providing a consistent visual style. ```javascript import { Auth } from '@supabase/auth-ui-react' import { // Import predefined theme ThemeSupa, } from '@supabase/auth-ui-shared' const supabase = createClient( '', '' ) const App = () => ( ) ``` -------------------------------- ### Update Supabase Auth Email Templates for `redirectTo` (HTML) Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/auth/redirect-urls.mdx This HTML example demonstrates how to adjust Supabase authentication email templates when utilizing the `redirectTo` option. It shows the modification of the `href` attribute in an anchor tag from `{{ .SiteURL }}` to `{{ .RedirectTo }}` to ensure that confirmation links correctly point to the post-authentication redirect location. ```html Confirm your mail Confirm your mail ``` -------------------------------- ### Initialize Supabase Client and Auth UI (JSX) Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/auth/quickstarts/react.mdx Sets up the Supabase client with your project URL and anon key, and configures the Auth component to handle user sessions. It displays the Auth UI when no session exists and a success message when logged in. ```jsx import './index.css' import { useState, useEffect } from 'react' import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js' import { Auth } from '@supabase/auth-ui-react' import { ThemeSupa } from '@supabase/auth-ui-shared' const supabase = createClient('https://.supabase.co', '') export default function App() { const [session, setSession] = useState(null) useEffect(() => { supabase.auth.getSession().then(({ data: { session } }) => { setSession(session) }) const { data: { subscription }, } = supabase.auth.onAuthStateChange((_event, session) => { setSession(session) }) return () => subscription.unsubscribe() }, []) if (!session) { return () } else { return (
Logged in!
) } } ``` -------------------------------- ### Manage SQL Permissions on Supabase Table Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/auth/auth-hooks/password-verification-hook.mdx This SQL snippet demonstrates how to grant all privileges on the `public.password_sign_in_attempts` table to the `supabase_auth_admin` role, and subsequently revoke all privileges from the `authenticated`, `anon`, and `public` roles. This is crucial for securing sensitive tables by restricting general access while allowing specific administrative functions. Ensure you have appropriate database superuser or ownership permissions to execute these commands. ```sql grant all on table public.password_sign_in_attempts to supabase_auth_admin; revoke all on table public.password_sign_in_attempts from authenticated, anon, public; ``` -------------------------------- ### Import and render Supabase Auth component in React Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/auth/auth-helpers/auth-ui.mdx This example demonstrates how to import the `Auth` component from `@supabase/auth-ui-react` and initialize a Supabase client. The `Auth` component is then rendered, passing the `supabaseClient` instance as a prop, creating a basic authentication interface without any specific styling. ```javascript import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js' import { Auth } from '@supabase/auth-ui-react' const supabase = createClient('', '') const App = () => ``` -------------------------------- ### Verify Supabase email OTP token for user authentication (TypeScript) Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/auth/auth-email-templates.mdx This TypeScript code uses `supabase.auth.verifyOtp` to authenticate a user with their email and a provided token. It's typically invoked on a client-side page where the user inputs the OTP after clicking an email link. Requires a `supabase` client instance and `email`, `token`, and `type` parameters. ```ts const { data, error } = await supabase.auth.verifyOtp({ email, token, type: 'email' }) ``` -------------------------------- ### Authenticate User for RLS Testing with pgTAP Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/local-development/testing/pgtap-extended.mdx This snippet utilizes `tests.authenticate_as` to simulate a user login, setting the current session's `auth.uid()` to that of a specified user role. This is essential for rigorously testing Row Level Security (RLS) policies and ensuring that data access and modification adhere to the defined permissions for different user types. ```SQL select tests.authenticate_as('free_author'); ``` -------------------------------- ### Customize Supabase Auth UI labels with localization.variables in JavaScript Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/auth/auth-helpers/auth-ui.mdx This snippet demonstrates how to initialize the Supabase client and render the `Auth` component from `@supabase/auth-ui-react`. It shows how to customize the email and password labels for the sign-in form using the `localization.variables` prop. This requires `createClient` from `@supabase/supabase-js` and `Auth` from `@supabase/auth-ui-react`. ```js import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js' import { Auth } from '@supabase/auth-ui-react' const supabase = createClient('', '') const App = () => ( ) ``` -------------------------------- ### Set Default Auth View in Supabase Auth UI (React) Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/auth/auth-helpers/auth-ui.mdx This example shows how to explicitly set the initial authentication view for the Supabase Auth UI component to either `sign_in` or `sign_up` using the `view` prop. This allows developers to control which form is presented first to the user. ```javascript ``` -------------------------------- ### Magic Link Email Template - HTML Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/auth/auth-email-passwordless.mdx Email template for PKCE flow Magic Link authentication. Contains personalized login link with token hash and email type parameter for server-side verification. ```html

Magic Link

Follow this link to login:

Log In

``` -------------------------------- ### Install Supabase Auth UI and client packages Source: https://github.com/supabase/supabase/blob/master/apps/docs/content/guides/auth/auth-helpers/auth-ui.mdx This command installs the necessary `@supabase/supabase-js`, `@supabase/auth-ui-react`, and `@supabase/auth-ui-shared` packages required to use the Auth UI component in a React application. ```bash npm install @supabase/supabase-js @supabase/auth-ui-react @supabase/auth-ui-shared ```
